evals.prompt: I have a repo with a Rust backend in `crates/api/` (axum, tests in `crates/api/tests/`), a React + TypeScript frontend in `web/src/` (Vite), and a `justfile` exposing `just build`, `just test`, and `just lint`. There is no CLAUDE.md and no .claude/rules/ yet. Set up project memory for this repo.
evals.expected_output: A hybrid scaffold: a lean root CLAUDE.md (with the /init prefix, the just commands, and big-picture architecture) plus at least one path-scoped .claude/rules/*.md for an area like the frontend or the api, presented as a tree + diffs for approval before writing.
assertions: Proposes a HYBRID layout: a root CLAUDE.md PLUS at least one .claude/rules/*.md file, not a single monolithic CLAUDE.md

The proposed root CLAUDE.md begins with the exact prefix line '# CLAUDE.md' immediately followed by 'This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.'
The root CLAUDE.md stays lean: repo-wide essentials only (the just build/test/lint commands and big-picture architecture), and does not duplicate the area-specific content that was moved into rules
At least one proposed .claude/rules/ file carries a paths: frontmatter glob targeting a real area such as web/src or crates/api
Does not write or apply any file before showing the proposed tree and diffs and asking the user for approval

evals.prompt: My repo keeps React components in `src/components/` (`.tsx`) where every component must use CSS modules and have an aria-label, and an API layer in `src/api/` (`.ts`) where every endpoint validates input with zod and returns the shared error envelope. No CLAUDE.md or rules exist yet. Scaffold the memory.
evals.expected_output: A lean CLAUDE.md plus two path-scoped rules: .claude/rules/frontend.md (paths: src/components/**/*.tsx) holding the component conventions, and .claude/rules/api.md (paths: src/api/**/*.ts) holding the endpoint conventions; globs verified against the described files.
assertions: Routes the component-specific conventions (CSS modules, aria-label) into a path-scoped rule rather than the root CLAUDE.md
The frontend rule's paths: glob targets the component files, e.g. 'src/components/**/*.tsx' or an equivalent that matches the described .tsx files
The API conventions land in a separate path-scoped rule with a glob such as 'src/api/**/*.ts'
States that each paths: glob must be (or has been) verified to match real files before finalizing
The root CLAUDE.md does not restate the per-area conventions that were moved into the rules

evals.prompt: My repo already has a 250-line CLAUDE.md that mixes build commands, top-level architecture, frontend styling rules, API conventions, and deployment steps all in one file. There are no .claude/rules/ yet. Run your init on it.
evals.expected_output: Detects the existing CLAUDE.md and, instead of recreating it, proposes keeping repo-wide essentials in CLAUDE.md while extracting the area-specific sections (frontend styling, API, deployment) into path-scoped rules, noting the file is over the ~200-line budget.
assertions: Detects the existing CLAUDE.md and does NOT overwrite or regenerate it from scratch
Switches to an improvement/split proposal: repo-wide essentials stay in CLAUDE.md while area-specific sections (frontend styling, API, deployment) are extracted into path-scoped rules
Preserves the substance of the existing content rather than discarding it, and does not leave content duplicated in both CLAUDE.md and a new rule
Notes that the file exceeds the ~200-line budget and that splitting into rules is the fix, or defers to the claude-md-improver skill for a full audit

evals.prompt: Set up CLAUDE.md for a small single-package Python CLI: it runs with `uv run mytool`, tests with `pytest`, and lints with `ruff`. Keep it tight.
evals.expected_output: A single lean CLAUDE.md (with the /init prefix and the real uv/pytest/ruff commands plus a one-line architecture note), no invented sections, no generic best-practice filler, and an explicit note that no path-scoped rules are warranted for a project this small.
assertions: Does not include generic best-practice filler such as 'write unit tests for all new code', 'provide helpful error messages', or 'never commit secrets / API keys'
Does not invent sections like 'Tips for Development', 'Common Development Tasks', or 'Support and Documentation' that were not derived from the repo
Does not enumerate every file or directory that is trivially discoverable
Includes the real project commands (uv run mytool, pytest, ruff) and only big-picture architecture
Recognizes that a single-area project may need only a lean CLAUDE.md with no rules, and says so rather than forcing a split

evals.prompt: Set up project memory for my Node service. package.json defines three test scripts — `test` (jest), `test:watch`, and `test:ci` (jest with coverage for CI) — and I haven't told you which one is the canonical command to document. There is no CLAUDE.md yet.
evals.expected_output: Recognizes that the canonical test command is ambiguous, asks one focused question (via AskUserQuestion) offering the candidate scripts with a sensible default rather than silently guessing, and proceeds to scaffold once the command is chosen or defaulted.
assertions: Recognizes that which test command is canonical cannot be determined from the repo alone and does not silently pick one as fact
Asks the user a focused question (e.g. via AskUserQuestion) listing the candidate test scripts rather than guessing
Keeps the questioning light: a single batched round with a sensible default, not an open-ended interview
Still produces the hybrid scaffold (lean CLAUDE.md plus any warranted rules) after the command is chosen or defaulted, without blocking indefinitely
